# Verified Autonomy

# Verified Autonomy

The **Verified Autonomy Pipeline** (VAP) is the core OCCP primitive. Every
task an agent accepts flows through five gates before any external side
effect is allowed:

1. **PLAN** — LLM drafts the steps it intends to take.
2. **VERIFY** — deterministic checks (schema, policy, cost, blast radius)
   run against the plan.
3. **APPROVE** — if policy requires human-in-the-loop (HITL), the task
   pauses for an operator. Low-risk actions auto-approve.
4. **EXECUTE** — the plan runs. Every tool call is observable via
   OpenTelemetry spans (`gen_ai.*` semantic conventions).
5. **AUDIT** — the complete chain (PLAN → VERIFY → APPROVE → EXECUTE
   results) is signed, hashed, and appended to a tamper-evident log.

## Why five gates

Autonomous agent incidents in production share a common shape: an agent
does "the right thing" for 99 inputs and something catastrophic for the
100th. The 2025 "$47k runaway loop" and the 2026 "procurement agent
double-ordering" postmortems both trace back to missing **policy** or
missing **audit**.

OCCP makes these two gates non-bypassable:

- The policy engine is **content-addressed** — changes to a rule require
  a new hash, and every gate decision logs which hash evaluated it.
- The audit log uses a Merkle chain — operators can independently
  verify that entries haven't been tampered with after the fact.

## Mapping to EU AI Act Art. 14

Article 14 requires human oversight that can:

1. **Understand** the system's capabilities → OCCP exposes every plan
   before execution.
2. **Intervene or halt** → the APPROVE gate is a pause point; the
   Kill Switch is a global halt.
3. **Override decisions** → rejected plans return to PLAN with the
   operator's rationale captured in the audit chain.

See [`.planning/EU_AI_ACT_ART14_COMPLIANCE_MAPPING.md`](https://github.com/azar-management-consulting/occp-core/blob/main/.planning/EU_AI_ACT_ART14_COMPLIANCE_MAPPING.md)
for the full compliance mapping.


# Your first agent — from zero to deployed

# Your first agent

This guide assumes you've finished the [Quickstart](/docs/quickstart) and
have a working API key.

## 1 · Scaffold the project

```bash
npx create-occp-app@latest my-agent --template hello-agent --ts
cd my-agent
npm install
```

Or, if you prefer a manual setup, clone the reference template:

```bash
git clone https://github.com/azar-management-consulting/occp-core
cp -r occp-core/templates/hello-agent my-agent
cd my-agent
```

## 2 · Wire credentials

```bash
cp .env.example .env
# edit .env → OCCP_API_KEY=occp_live_sk_...
```

## 3 · Run it

```bash
npm start
```

Expected output:

```
[hello-agent] sending task to https://api.occp.ai ...
[hello-agent] pipeline result: pass
[hello-agent] output: Hello from your first OCCP agent ✓
```

## 4 · Add a tool

Drop a file under `src/tools/` using the `echo.js` shape:

```js
export const myTool = {
  name: "search_docs",
  description: "Search OCCP documentation",
  parameters: {
    type: "object",
    properties: { query: { type: "string" } },
    required: ["query"],
  },
  async run({ query }) {
    const response = await fetch(
      `https://docs.occp.ai/api/search?q=${encodeURIComponent(query)}`,
    );
    return response.json();
  },
};
```

## 5 · Deploy

Push the repo to GitHub and click **Deploy to OCCP** from the dashboard.
The workflow in `.github/workflows/deploy.yml` handles the rest.

## Further reading

- [Writing policies](/docs/guides/policy-writing)
- [MCP integration](/docs/mcp)
- [Audit chain verification](/docs/concepts/verified-autonomy)


# OCCP Docs

# Get started in 60 seconds

OCCP is the open-source control plane for autonomous AI agents. Every
action your agent takes is **planned, policy-gated, executed, validated,
and audit-logged** — all before the agent "commits" anything irreversible.

## Install

```bash
pip install occp
# or
npm install @occp/sdk
```

## Your first verified run

```python
from occp import ControlPlane

cp = ControlPlane(policy="policies/finance.rego")

result = cp.run(
    agent="claude-opus-4-7",
    task="Refund order #4521 if return window is open",
)

# PLAN → VERIFY → APPROVE → EXECUTE → AUDIT
print(result.audit_chain_id)
```

## Next

- [Quickstart — 5 minute walkthrough](/docs/quickstart)
- [Core concepts](/docs/concepts)
- [Guides](/docs/guides)
- [API reference](/docs/api-reference)


# Quickstart

# Quickstart

This page walks you from zero to your first successful **PLAN → VERIFY →
APPROVE → EXECUTE → AUDIT** cycle in under 5 minutes.

## 1 · Sign in

Click [Start free on the dashboard](https://dash.occp.ai/onboarding/start)
and authenticate with GitHub.

> OAuth is the recommended path. No credit card. No email verification
> step blocks you — that happens async.

## 2 · Generate an API key

From the onboarding wizard, click **Generate your first API key**. The
key is shown only once and starts with `occp_live_sk_`.

Save it to a `.env` file:

```bash
OCCP_API_KEY=occp_live_sk_xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx
```

## 3 · Install the SDK

```bash
# Python
pip install occp

# Node / TypeScript
npm install @occp/sdk
```

## 4 · Run a verified task

```python
from occp import ControlPlane
import os

cp = ControlPlane(api_key=os.environ["OCCP_API_KEY"])

result = cp.run(
    agent="claude-opus-4-7",
    task="Say hello and confirm the pipeline is working.",
)

print(result.status)          # 'pass'
print(result.audit_chain_id)  # tamper-evident chain id
```

## 5 · View the audit trail

Head to the [Audit page](https://dash.occp.ai/audit) on the dashboard.
Your run is listed there with signed evidence for every stage.

Done — you've just run a fully governed autonomous action.

## What to read next

- [Verified Autonomy — core concept](/docs/concepts/verified-autonomy)
- [Writing policies](/docs/guides/policy-writing)
- [Deploying agents via GitHub](/docs/guides/first-agent)
